title: Monetary Policy Report—April 2025
summary: The Bank of Canada released its April 2025 Monetary Policy Report, maintaining the overnight rate target at 2.75% (policy rate at 2¾%). The report notes that while the Canadian economy ended 2024 strong with inflation near the 2% target, escalating US tariffs and trade uncertainty have significantly diminished growth prospects and introduced upward price pressures. The Bank presents illustrative scenarios given the unpredictability of US trade policy.
key_points: Bank of Canada held target overnight rate at 2.75%, with Bank Rate at 3% and deposit rate at 2.70%
Canadian economy ended 2024 strong, but trade conflict is diminishing growth prospects
Inflation was near the 2% target in Q4 2024 before the intensification of trade conflict
US has threatened, imposed, and suspended tariffs on Canada, with many tariffs remaining in place
Removal of consumer carbon tax will temporarily lower inflation with a one-time reduction in consumer prices, mostly gasoline
Canada's potential output growth is anticipated to slow due to declining population growth and trade conflict
Range for Canada's neutral interest rate lies between 2.25% and 3.25%
why_it_matters: This quarterly monetary policy report from the Bank of Canada provides the official institutional outlook on Canadian economic conditions and inflation trajectory. The report signals concern about trade conflict impacts on growth while noting offsetting effects from carbon tax removal. Investors in Canadian equities, bonds, and CAD should monitor how these competing forces—tariff-driven price pressures versus weaker growth—affect the Bank's future rate path.
macro_context: The report presents a highly uncertain outlook driven by US trade policy unpredictability. Tariffs are expected to slow growth and add price pressures, while carbon tax removal will temporarily lower inflation. The Bank emphasizes that the speed and magnitude of US policy shifts make economic forecasting particularly difficult. Global economic growth was solid at end-2024 with inflation easing toward central bank targets before US tariff imposition.

issues_found: market_impact.confidence is set to 0 instead of being estimated—this is a data quality issue but not a factual error in the summary itself
